title: add a SIF systemtest
body:
To make sure that the basic functionality is exercised in Skopeo and
c/image CI.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
> I wonder a bit about the podman run part — wouldn’t it break on non-x86 architectures?
>
> (Alternatively, maybe we could just copy sif: to dir:, and inspect the tar file to see that a specific file exists.)

Yes, the `run` indeed was already causing issues.  I'll go with dir + inspect.  We can re-iterate in the future.
My workday is about to end, so I used a shortcut to only look for the architecture.  The digests (also of the layer) are different on each copy which makes testing a bit harder.

> Heads up to @lsm5 and @jnovy that this will change the `skopeo-tests` package from `noarch` to `<arch>`. I don't think specfile changes will be necessary, because I see `cp -pav` in both Fedora and RHEL, but please be alert on the next build.

I think it can remain `noarch` since the sif image is only copied, not being run in a container.
